key_insight: A fourth-order 3D singularly perturbed problem is decoupled into simpler second-order subproblems plus a Stokes-type system, enabling a robust low-order finite element treatment.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, parameter-robust discretization strategy for challenging thin-layer or boundary-layer dominated solid mechanics and PDE models in 3D.
method: Use generalized Helmholtz decomposition to split the original fourth-order problem.
Solve two second-order elliptic problems with standard linear finite elements.
Handle the remaining curl-constrained Stokes-type system with the MINI element plus an added L2 Lagrange-multiplier term for robustness.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a uniform error estimate of order h^{1/2} with respect to the perturbation parameter and states that numerical experiments support the theory.
watch_item: The proved convergence rate is only h^{1/2}, so achieving high accuracy may require substantial mesh refinement despite the robustness.
paper_title: A Decoupled Low-Order Conforming Mixed Finite Element Method for a Three-Dimensional Fourth-Order Singularly Perturbed Problem
